children.heading: Chiefs
children.label: 1 Chiefs
children.text: The term “Chiefs” means the Chief of the Forest Service and the Chief of the Natural Resources Conservation Service.

children.label: A
children.text: to reduce the risk of wildfire;

children.label: B
children.text: to protect water quality and supply; or

children.label: C
children.text: to improve wildlife habitat for at-risk species.

children.heading: Program
children.label: 3 Program
children.text: The term “Program” means the Joint Chiefs Landscape Restoration Partnership program established under subsection (b)(1).

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 4 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Agriculture.

children.heading: Wildland-urban interface
children.label: 5 Wildland-urban interface
children.text: The term “wildland-urban interface” has the meaning given the term in .section 6511 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a Joint Chiefs Landscape Restoration Partnership program to improve the health and resilience of forest landscapes across National Forest System land and State, Tribal, and private land.

children.label: A
children.text: assisting producers and landowners in implementing eligible activities on eligible private or Tribal land using the applicable programs and authorities administered by the Chief of the Natural Resources Conservation Service under title XII of the Food Security Act of 1985 ( et seq.), not including the conservation reserve program established under subchapter B of chapter 1 of subtitle D of that title ( et seq.); and16 U.S.C. 380116 U.S.C. 3831

children.label: B
children.text: conducting eligible activities on National Forest System land or assisting landowners in implementing eligible activities on State, Tribal, or private land using the applicable programs and authorities administered by the Chief of the Forest Service.

children.heading: Selection of eligible activities
children.label: c Selection of eligible activities
children.text: The appropriate Regional Forester and State Conservationist shall jointly submit to the Chiefs on an annual basis proposals for eligible activities under the Program.

children.label: A
children.text: reduces wildfire risk in a municipal watershed or the wildland-urban interface;

children.label: B
children.text: was developed through a collaborative process with participation from diverse stakeholders;

children.label: C
children.text: increases forest workforce capacity or forest business infrastructure and development;

children.label: D
children.text: leverages existing authorities and non-Federal funding;

children.label: E
children.text: provides measurable outcomes; or

children.label: F
children.text: supports established State and regional priorities; and
such other criteria relating to the merits of the proposals as the Chiefs determine to be appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: the solicitation of proposals under subsection (c); and

children.label: B
children.text: the criteria for selecting proposals in accordance with subsection (d); and
information relating to the Program and activities funded under the Program to States, Indian Tribes, units of local government, and private landowners.
in a wilderness area or designated wilderness study area;
in an inventoried roadless area;
on any Federal land on which, by Act of Congress or Presidential proclamation, the removal of vegetation is restricted or prohibited; or
in an area in which the eligible activity would be inconsistent with the applicable land and resource management plan.

children.label: A
children.text: funding mechanisms for the Program;

children.label: B
children.text: staff capacity to carry out the Program;

children.label: C
children.text: privacy laws applicable to the Program;

children.label: D
children.text: data collection under the Program;

children.label: E
children.text: monitoring and outcomes under the Program; and

children.label: F
children.text: such other matters as the Secretary considers to be appropriate.

children.heading: Additional reports
children.label: 2 Additional reports
children.text: For each of fiscal years 2022 and 2023, the Chiefs shall submit to the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry and the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate and the Committee on Agriculture and the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives a report describing projects for which funding is provided under the Program, including the status and outcomes of those projects.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 1 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out the Program $90,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2022 and 2023.

children.heading: Additional funds
children.label: 2 Additional funds
children.text: In addition to the funds described in paragraph (1), the Secretary may obligate available funds from accounts used to carry out the existing Joint Chiefs’ Landscape Restoration Partnership prior to , to carry out the Program.November 15, 2021

children.heading: Duration of availability
children.label: 3 Duration of availability
children.text: Funds made available under paragraph (1) shall remain available until expended.

children.label: A
children.text: not less than 40 percent shall be allocated to carry out eligible activities through the Natural Resources Conservation Service;

children.label: B
children.text: not less than 40 percent shall be allocated to carry out eligible activities through the Forest Service; and

children.label: i
children.text: to carry out eligible activities; or

children.label: ii
children.text: for other purposes, such as technical assistance, project development, or local capacity building.
